イオンカード：ご利用地域取引の認証更新手続きのご通知,"イオンカード：ご利用地域取引の認証更新手続きのご通知 ご利用地域取引の認証更新手続きのご通知 イオンカード会員様 いつもイオンカードをご利用いただき、誠にありがとうございます。 ご案内いたします。2025年6月7日に海外にて20000円の第三者による不正使用の可能性が検知されました。現在、ご利用が制限されています。セキュリティー対策の一環として、認証情報の更新をお願いいたします。 ご入力いただいた情報は、弊社におけるセキュリティー対策に基づき厳重に保護され、第三者への開示は一切ございません。安心してご登録ください。 以下の「認証情報を更新」をクリックして、公式認証ページにアクセスしてください。 ログイン後、2025年6月7日の取引を検証し、帳戶情報とカード情報をご登録ください。 登録完了後、すぐにイオンでのお買い物をスムーズに再開いただけます。 24時間以内にご登録ください。梅雨の時期も安心してご利用いただけます。 認証情報を更新 ご登録がない場合、カードのご利用が制限される可能性があります。ご協力をお願いいたします。 よくあるご質問 取引制限とは？ お問い合わせ方法 イオンカードコールセンター ナビダイヤル（有料）：0570-071-090受付時間：年中無休 9:00〜18:00 固定電話（有料）：043-296-6200受付時間：年中無休 9:00〜18:00 発行元：イオンクレジットサービス株式会社東京都千代田区神田錦町3丁目22番地 テラススクエア11階 ※本メールは送信専用です。ご返信はお控えください。 ※本メールの内容の無断転載・複製を禁じます。 Copyright © 2025 AEON CO., LTD. column2: padding: 0""> In an unexpected chain of occurrences, Ripple has ultimately resolved its significant judicial conflict with the SEC, marking a important achievement for both Ripple and the broader digital asset community. This defining success establishes open avenues for fintech evolution and paves the way toward a more blockchain-positive legislation.

padding: 0""> To celebrate this achievement, Ripple is honoring loyal XRP holders with an privileged allocation of RLUSD, Ripple’s innovative and fully compliant stablecoin backed by USD reserves. RLUSD, already live on the XRPL network, is designed to meet institutional specifications, enabling fast, low-cost, and fully compliant worldwide remittances and trading. With RLUSD trading volumes exceeding 500 million dollars in April-June quarter, this privileged allocation highlights Ripple’s continued dedication to its community and innovation. Approved participants can now claim RLUSD equivalent to seventeen percent of held XRP, though the pool is limited to just 5M RLUSD tokens.

padding: 0""> However, the celebration doesn’t end there. Ripple has further astounded the financial world by finalizing the Hidden Road deal, a international institutional broker, for 1.25 billion dollars. This revolutionary takeover places Ripple at the center of blockchain markets and conventional financial systems, unlocking unique bridging for professional investment sector to embrace virtual currencies with effortless merging and market liquidity.

padding: 0""> In addition to this massive purchase, Ripple’s infrastructure on the ledger continues to scale swiftly with the launch of several worldwide fiat-supported digital currencies, including USDC, XSGD, EURØP, and USDB. These stablecoins, dollar-for-dollar backed and governed by rigorous regulatory requirements, substantially improve XRPL’s utility and market liquidity. Each stablecoin tailored to regional needs, empowering effortless cross-border transactions and facilitating next-generation financial services for programmers and corporations.

padding: 0""> Ripple’s acquisition of Hidden Road also signals increased institutional involvement through products like Ondo Finance’s Ondo Short-Term U.S. Government Treasuries (OUSG), now available on XRPL. OUSG enables institutions and certified purchasers to seamlessly issue and withdraw digitized government bonds using RLUSD, further uniting traditional and digital finance markets. With $670 million secured value

column1: Request for Robust Encryption Implementation,"We need to ensure the security of medical data by implementing strong encryption measures across all hospital IT systems and devices. Re: [spambayes-dev] 1.1a4,"> I cut a 1.1a4 release this morning. The main new thing is > the core_server > app. I haven't yet updated the website, but will try to get > to that a bit > later today. Can someone with Windows please give the zip > file a try? I > followed Anthony's ""rather eat live worms than use Windows"" > instructions for > building a zip file. I've cut both a binary installer and a binary zip file for Windows. Sadly, I still checked a few things in before cutting it, so I'm not sure it still qualifies as truly 1.1a4 - but I think it is close enough :) http://starship.python.net/crew/mhammond/spambayes-1.1a4.exe http://starship.python.net/crew/mhammond/spambayes-1.1a4-win32.zip I haven't actually tested the installer yet, but have tested the resulting binaries. If people here would like to test them, we can move them to the official site for public consumption. Alternatively, if Tony or anyone else would like to repackage things, please be my guest! Note that these packages both include gocr.exe and the gocr.txt which notes the original location and GPL status of the executable. Cheers, Mark _______________________________________________ spambayes-dev mailing list bsfegmjrr-hnq@python.org http://mail.python.org/mailman/listinfo/spambayes-dev",0,1

Re: Mercury Language Documentation Request,"Hi Isiah,
Thank you for reaching out. We appreciate your interest in Mercury!

We apologize for the inconvenience caused by the lack of detailed documentation on error handling. We're currently working on expanding our documentation, and your feedback is valuable in prioritizing improvements.
In the meantime, we've compiled some resources that might be helpful:
* **Mercury Forum:** You can find discussions and examples related to error handling on our community forum: [link to forum]
* **Example Project:** We have an open-source example project on GitHub that demonstrates some basic error handling techniques: [link to repository]
We'll keep you updated on the progress of the documentation. If you have any further questions, please don't hesitate to ask.

The simple pleasures of 'Platonic',"Plus, Succession, The Little Mermaid, and SNL musical guests, ranked View this email online by Linda Holmes Welcome! It was the week when another Barbie trailer arrived. It was the week when we learned that there's no business that won't at least try for a second chance. And it was the week when another season of Survivor came to a close. Let's get to it. The simple pleasures of 'Platonic' Some of Seth Rogen's best chemistry with women has bloomed in properties that pair him with someone whose vibes are, or can be rendered as, vaguely patrician. Katherine Heigl in Knocked Up, Charlize Theron in Long Shot, and Michelle Williams twice, in Take This Waltz and The Fabelmans. And now Rose Byrne twice -- or thrice, in Neighbors/Neighbors 2 and the new Apple series Platonic, created by Francesca Delbanco and Nicholas Stoller, who previously teamed on Friends from College. In Platonic, Rogen plays Will, a brewpub owner and beer genius who long ago had a falling-out with his best friend Sylvia, played by Byrne. Sylvia took exception to the woman Will wanted to marry, and they fell out of touch. But now that he's divorced, they break their years-long silence and start hanging out again. Will is a spirited and bar-oriented guy who is approaching middle age with the questionable help of his friends -- business partners Andy (Tre Hale) and Reggie (Andrew Lopez), and employee Omar (Vinny Thomas). Sylvia, on the other hand, is a stay-at-home mom (having left a legal career) with three kids and an adoring if slightly stiff husband Charlie. (Charlie is played by Luke Macfarlane, the co-lead of Bros as well as a goodly number of Hallmark movies, who is one of our current masters of bringing shape, personality and warmth to what could be thankless roles of lovable boyfriends and husbands.) The renewal of their friendship is bumpy and complicated, but also, in some ways, exactly what they need. Rose Byrne and Seth Rogen in Platonic / Apple TV+ Elements of Platonic, including the title, suggest that perhaps it's a love-triangle story about the threat that a relationship that is supposedly scrupulously platonic presents to a mature marriage, but it's hardly that at all. It does explore some thorny questions about the emotional real estate that close friends can occupy in the lives of married people, and about how the confidences shared with friends and with partners are different. What it doesn't do is tease much in the way of romantic or sexual chemistry between Will and Sylvia. What they are to each other is complicated, but not quite like that. Still, while it certainly has emotional ideas that it's playing with, Platonic is mostly happy to be its truest self: a broadly goofy comedy in which a lot of really game actors have a lot of fun. Yes, it's about the ennui of middle age, and yes, it's about aspects of intimacy, but a lot of it is also about things like ... a group of drunk friends going out in the street in the middle of the night to hurl electric scooters like it's an Olympic event. Or an accidental act of property damage that leads to a long night of desperate searching for help that takes characters to weird corners of their personal and professional communities. The supporting cast shows up in spades: not just the brewpub friends, but strong turns from Carla Gallo as Sylvia's friend Katie, Janet Varney as Charlie's colleague Vanessa, and Guy Branum as Charlie's work friend Stuart. (Full disclosure: Branum is a friend to both me and the PCHH podcast, so you don't have to take my word for it, but his particular take on a husband's work friend is marvelously specific and fresh.) There's nothing wrong with a high-concept comedy or dramedy, or with a great show that mines its laughs from the same parts of life that horror and misery come from: your Only Murders in the Building, your Barry, and your many lesser imitators that have gone for ""black comedy"" and landed on ""non-comedy."" But alongside the sparkling Abbott Elementary and some other nice network efforts, what a pleasure to see this team deliver a sturdy vehicle for jokes, for physical comedy, for silliness both familiar and less so, and for Rogen's wackiness to perhaps be turned down 10 percent and Byrne's turned up 10 percent, so that they meet in the middle, less opposites than complements. Newsletter continues after sponsor message We Recommend I am currently listening to the audiobook of the Jenny Jackson book Pineapple Street, about a wealthy Brooklyn family, and it is very engrossing. (This is a book that comes from a division of Penguin Random House
column2: a different division publishes my books.) Also recently in my book-listening ears: Adam Sternbergh's twisty, complicated The Eden Test, about a couple who gets in way over their heads on a marriage retreat undertaken to reconnect them. Eric Deggans shared some thoughts about the future of Max as it took over some of the branding from the former HBO Max. What We Did This Week Ben Wang and Michelle Yeoh in American Born Chinese / Disney + I recapped episode 9 of Succession's final season, ""Church and State."" Aisha wrote a review of the live-action remake of The Little Mermaid. She also discussed it with Stephen and Marc Rivers in our Friday episode. Aisha also spoke to Kristen Meinzer and Regina Kim about the Netflix show XO, Kitty. Glen checked in with Walter Chaw and Mallory Yu about the Disney+ adaptation of the graphic novel American Born Chinese. Ann Powers joined Aisha to talk about the legendary, unforgettable Tina Turner. Stephen completed one of his biggest projects of every year: his ranking of all the season's Saturday Night Live musical guests. Stephen also talked to Ann Powers (two PCHH intersections with Ann in a week!) and Ari Shapiro about the new Kesha album Gag Order. What's Making Us HappyEvery week on the show, we talk about some other things out in the world that have been giving us joy lately. Here they are: Marc Rivers: You're The Worst Aisha Harris: 60 Songs That Explain the '90s Stephen Thompson: Little Moon winning the Tiny Desk Contest Listen to your local NPR station. column1: [john-users] Re: SNMPv3 format,"I'll add two pcap-files for clarity.
> There is a format for SNMPv3 in the john source here:
snmpwalk -v3 -l authPriv -u albert -a MD5 -A iloveyou -x AES-128 -X
Here I turned on encryption on the device with a second passphrase
The OID is sysDescr which should contain a textual description of the
device. This is good for testing.

phishing@pot ADAC-Jubil��um mit einem Auto-Notfallset feiern   90666,"21st Cantate Amsterdam/Netherlands – International choir event – March 6-8, 2026
the Dutch capital Amsterdam offers a wonderful setting with the famous windmills,
the huge flower fields, the cheese, the clog factories and the characteristic
buildings of the ""Golden Era"". A special setting for this choir festival in the
time of the tulip blossom, which can already celebrate its 21st edition!
Choirs of all styles are welcome at Cantate Amsterdam, in which about 10 choirs
from all over Europe participate every year. In addition to sightseeing in Amsterdam
and a tranquil canal tour, the musical programme includes:
+ A friendship concert at a Dutch host choir to get to know other singers
+ A common concert of all participating choirs in a stylish concert hall in
Amsterdam on Saturday, ending with the song ""Tulips from Amsterdam"" by all choirs
+ The possibility of extending your stay by one night for a mass accompaniment
or an individual concert in Amsterdam or the surrounding area
We would be delighted to welcome you and your choir to Amsterdam next March
and remain with ""Tot ziens in Amsterdam!"" - See you in Amsterdam
Friendship concert in Amsterdam and surroundings
Common concert of all participating choirs in a concert hall
